label: SES-140 / CNV-042 close-out — WTK-002 ADO structural decomposer. Builds the decomposer (design §3.2.1/§4.1): POST /planning-items/{id}/decompose creates all six phase Workstreams (Planned) chained by serial blocked_by gates. DEC-362 records the once-only, all-phases, serial-chain interface. WTK-002 (under WSK-001) driven to Complete; ADDRESSES (not resolves) PI-114 under PRJ-018. Code on main as commit 8f70e3e.

session.session_executive_summary: Claude Code execution session building WTK-002, the ADO structural decomposer — the second Work Task of PI-114's Development phase (WSK-001). Implements design §3.2.1/§4.1: a new access module repositories/decomposition.py (decompose_planning_item, existing_phase_workstreams, PHASE_SEQUENCE) and a POST /planning-items/{id}/decompose endpoint that, given a Planning Item, creates all six phase Workstreams (Architecture..Deployment) in Planned status, wires each one's workstream_belongs_to_planning_item edge, and chains consecutive phases with serial blocked_by gates. Once-only (409 on re-decompose), 404 for an unknown PI. Added access + API tests; full v2 suite green. DEC-362 records the interface. WTK-002 driven to Complete; addresses (not resolves) PI-114.
session.session_description: Execution session in the same Claude Code thread that closed SES-139, picking up the next PI-114 increment chosen by Doug: the structural decomposer. WTK-002 was created via direct POST /work-tasks (Workstreams/Work Tasks are operational entities written directly, never bundled into close-out payloads), set under WSK-001 (the PI-114 Development phase) via work_task_belongs_to_workstream, and driven through its full lifecycle Planned -> Ready -> Claimed(CNV-042) -> In Progress -> Complete. The build added repositories/decomposition.py with PHASE_SEQUENCE (the canonical Architecture..Deployment order, design §4.1/decision 8), decompose_planning_item (creates all six Workstreams via the concurrency-safe autoassign path, then wires the belongs_to_planning_item and serial blocked_by edges after — each phase blocked_by the immediately prior one, §5 decision 5), and existing_phase_workstreams (the once-only guard's query). Exposed as POST /planning-items/{id}/decompose on the planning_items router (201 with the created Workstreams; 409 if already decomposed; 404 for unknown PI). Tests: test_decomposition.py (6 phases in order, belongs + blocked_by chain, once-only guard, unknown-PI NotFound) and test_decompose_route.py (the endpoint, the chain via /references, once-only 409, unknown 404). Full v2 suite green. DEC-362 records the interface decision. The new endpoint goes live on the next API restart; this close-out does not require invoking it. The decomposer does no scope judgment — the phase specialists (next Work Tasks) consume these Planned Workstreams, scoping each into Work Tasks or asserting Not Applicable. Addresses PI-114 without resolving it.

commits.commit_message_first_line: v2: WTK-002 — ADO structural decomposer (PI -> six phase Workstreams)
commits.commit_message_full:
v2: WTK-002 — ADO structural decomposer (PI -> six phase Workstreams)

Implements the ADO structural step (design §3.2.1 / §4.1): given a
Planning Item, create every phase Workstream (Architecture, Development,
Testing, Documentation, Data Migration, Deployment) in Planned status,
wire each one's workstream_belongs_to_planning_item edge, and chain
consecutive phases with serial blocked_by gates (decision 5).

- access/repositories/decomposition.py: PHASE_SEQUENCE,
  decompose_planning_item (concurrency-safe autoassign then edge-wire),
  existing_phase_workstreams (the once-only guard's query).
- POST /planning-items/{id}/decompose: 201 with the created Workstreams;
  409 if already decomposed (not idempotent — a re-decompose is a
  double-plan bug, surfaced not masked); 404 for an unknown PI.
- Tests: test_decomposition.py + test_decompose_route.py.

The decomposer makes no scope judgment — phases are born Planned/empty;
the phase specialists scope them next (Work Tasks, or Not Applicable).
Full v2 suite green (2249 passed). DEC-362 records the interface.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.8 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

decisions.title: ADO structural decomposer is a once-only, all-phases, serial-blocked_by step at POST /planning-items/{id}/decompose
decisions.context: Design §3.2.1 / §4.1 specify the ADO structural step — create every phase Workstream for a Planning Item, with scope judgment deferred to the phase specialists — but left the implementation shape open: where the function lives, the endpoint, idempotency, and how the phase gates are wired. WTK-002 had to settle these while building it.
decisions.decision: A dedicated access module repositories/decomposition.py (decompose_planning_item, existing_phase_workstreams, PHASE_SEQUENCE) exposed as POST /planning-items/{id}/decompose (the PI is the subject, so it reads naturally on the planning_items router). It always creates all six phase Workstreams in the canonical Architecture->Development->Testing->Documentation->Data Migration->Deployment order (decision 8), each in Planned status with a workstream_belongs_to_planning_item edge, and chains consecutive phases with a single serial blocked_by edge (phase N blocked_by phase N-1) per decision 5 (serial phases within a PI). Workstreams are created via the concurrency-safe autoassign path and the edges wired afterward (not explicit-id inline). It is **once-only, not idempotent**: if the PI already has any phase Workstream it raises 409, because re-decomposition would double-plan — re-scoping adds Work Tasks within the existing phases (§4.6), it does not recreate phases.
decisions.alternatives_considered: Idempotent upsert (return the existing Workstreams on re-call) — rejected: it silently masks a double-plan bug, whereas the once-only 409 surfaces it. Placing the endpoint on the workstreams router (e.g. POST /workstreams/decompose?pi=NNN) — rejected: the Planning Item is the subject of decomposition, so POST /planning-items/{id}/decompose is the correct resource. Wiring blocked_by as a richer DAG by area/layer rank at this step — rejected: phases are serial within a PI (decision 5), so a linear chain is exactly right; intra-phase parallelism is a Work-Task-level concern the phase specialists express, not the structural step.
decisions.consequences: New module repositories/decomposition.py and a new endpoint; tests in test_decomposition.py + test_decompose_route.py. The decomposer does no scope judgment — every phase is born Planned and empty; the phase specialists (the next Work Tasks under WSK-001) consume them, scoping each into Work Tasks or asserting Not Applicable (§4.3). The serial blocked_by chain gives the PI Lead unambiguous gate signals (§3.3/§5). The endpoint is reachable only after the running API restarts onto the new code.
decisions.executive_summary: While building the ADO structural decomposer (WTK-002), this decision settles its interface: a once-only POST /planning-items/{id}/decompose that always creates all six phase Workstreams (Planned, canonical order) chained by serial blocked_by gates, with each phase's belongs_to_planning_item edge wired. It is deliberately not idempotent — a re-decompose 409s, surfacing a double-plan bug rather than masking it, since re-scoping adds Work Tasks within phases and never recreates them. The decomposer makes no scope judgment; the phase specialists do that next, marking empty phases Not Applicable. Lives in repositories/decomposition.py with access + API tests.
